position_summary: The novel hypothesis remains an open empirical question and is not inherently circular: fixed 2025 Perturb-seq evidence and post-2016 Europe PMC labels are distinct signals. However, the implementation plan is not yet a defensible measurement spec because its ranking population, Wayfinder comparator, gate reconstruction, inference procedure, and reproducibility contract are materially underspecified or incorrect against the repository code.

findings.title: Ranking is not conditioned on the stated novel-at-T population
findings.evidence: The artifact’s research question says “Among ... links that were novel as of T,” but Build step 3 merely labels `novel-at-T ∧ established-now` as positive and step 4 ranks the entire as-of-T-eligible frame. Thus candidates with `ac_lit_asof > 1` remain rankable negatives. `propose.py` has no novelty eligibility gate: it gates on `ab`, `bc`, and `ac_known`, then computes `ac_lit` only after referee culling.
findings.concern_path: The evaluation becomes prediction of a joint event—being rare at T and later established—rather than predicting establishment conditional on novelty. B-lit-rarity can win by recovering the label’s novelty component, while Wayfinder’s score also directly penalizes `ac_lit`; precision@k then does not answer the declared question.
findings.suggested_action: Filter the common comparison frame to `ac_lit_asof <= 1` before ranking, then define positives solely as post-T establishment within that frame.

findings.title: “Wayfinder = referee-supported-first” is not the implemented proposer and creates an ill-defined comparison
findings.evidence: `propose.py` gates 22,039 pairs, runs `referee_triple`, culls all but `supported`, `supported_flagged`, and `supported_weak`, and only then fetches `ac_lit` and scores the 43 survivors. The stage-1 JSON confirms 22,039 eligible pairs, 21,995 `refuted_for_c`, and only 43 retained. The plan instead says every method ranks the identical eligible set and describes Wayfinder as “referee-supported-first, then score,” without defining scores or ordering for the roughly 21,996 non-retained rows.
findings.concern_path: An implementation may silently compare Wayfinder on 43 candidates against baselines on 22,039, assign unsupported rows arbitrary tail order, or reimplement a new supported-first ranker that is not the repository’s Wayfinder method. Each choice produces different precision, MAP, and recall.
findings.suggested_action: Specify one exact total-order function over the same novel-at-T frame, including verdict-class ordering, unsupported-row tie-breaking, and score computation, and label it explicitly as an evaluation variant if it differs from `propose.py`.

findings.title: The as-of-T gate is not guaranteed to “only shrink” the frame
findings.evidence: Build step 2 and the spike caveat claim the as-of-T `ab` gate can only shrink eligibility. In `propose.py`, however, `ab_gate` is recomputed as a percentile of the full `ab` distribution: `sorted(ab.values())[round(ab_gate_pct*(len(ab)-1))]`. Recomputing all counts at T changes both the threshold and membership; approximately half the genes still pass, but a currently passing gene can fail and a currently failing gene can pass. Dropping the `ac_known <= tau` gate also expands the frame.
findings.concern_path: The projected 1,469/22,039 class size and “shrink-only” checkpoint do not bound the actual sliced population. Composition can shift non-monotonically, invalidating the spike’s power projection and potentially changing disease/gene prevalence substantially.
findings.suggested_action: Delete the monotonic-shrink assertion and require full reconstruction and reporting of the sliced frame, overlap with the current frame, and exact positive count before analysis.

findings.title: The spike does not validate the planned sample frame or positive count
findings.evidence: The spike sampled only 150 pairs from the current 22,039-pair headline frame, which used current `ab`, current `bc`, and current `ac_known <= 0.1` (`heldout-eval-feasibility`, lines 18–24 and 89–93). Its 1,469 positives are a projection from 10 sampled positives, not a measured count. The plan changes all three literature gates and drops `ac_known`.
findings.concern_path: The advertised ~6.7% prevalence and CI floor of 807 may bear little relation to the actual novel-at-T, referee-stratified frame. With only 43 current referee survivors, top-rank performance may also have far less effective information than the projected global positive count suggests.
findings.suggested_action: Treat 1,469 as preliminary only and make an exact full-frame enumeration—not a threshold-adjustable checkpoint—the prerequisite for freezing the analysis protocol.

findings.title: Bootstrap and non-overlapping-CI acceptance rule are statistically unsound for paired rankings
findings.evidence: Build step 5 proposes row-resampling the candidate frame for precision@k/MAP/recall, while acceptance gate (b) requires non-overlapping marginal CIs. Methods are evaluated on the same candidates and their contrast is paired; candidate rows also share genes and diseases, violating an independent-row bootstrap assumption. Non-overlap of two 95% CIs is not the correct test or CI for their difference.
findings.concern_path: Reported uncertainty can be too narrow because repeated gene-level and disease-level structure is ignored, while the non-overlap rule can misclassify a meaningful paired difference as null or encourage post hoc threshold choices until intervals separate.
findings.suggested_action: Predefine paired method-difference intervals with gene-aware or two-way clustered resampling and remove non-overlapping marginal CIs as an acceptance criterion.

findings.title: Primary metric and cutoff are not actually prespecified
findings.evidence: The plan calls “precision@k” primary while listing k=5,10,20,50, also uses k for the `ac_lit_now >= 5` label threshold, and acceptance gate (b) says precision@k separation without selecting one cutoff. MAP and recall@N are listed, but N is undefined; PR-AUC is omitted despite the acknowledged class imbalance.
findings.concern_path: After seeing results, any of four precision cutoffs, label thresholds, novelty thresholds, T values, or metrics can become the headline. This multiplicity makes a nominally positive result non-decision-quality even if every computation is correct.
findings.suggested_action: Freeze one primary estimand and rank cutoff, rename the establishment threshold, define all secondary metrics, and specify multiplicity handling before running the full labels.

findings.title: Dropping `ac_known` is defensible for leakage avoidance but changes the target method and population
findings.evidence: In `propose.py`, `ac_known` is both an eligibility gate (`<= tau`) and a score penalty (`-w2 * ac_known`). The plan removes both by dropping the gate and forcing the score term to zero. The spike, however, retained the current gate. Applying the removal symmetrically to baselines does not make the resulting Wayfinder ranking equivalent to the implemented method.
findings.concern_path: Current Open Targets may indeed leak later knowledge and bias against positives, but removing it may admit many already-known-at-T associations not captured by sparse Europe PMC co-mentions and can change disease-specific composition. The experiment then measures an ablated Wayfinder variant, not simply a clean time slice of Wayfinder.
findings.suggested_action: Name this as an `ac_known`-ablated variant and report sensitivity analyses with current-OT exclusion clearly labeled as leaky/conservative rather than claiming method identity.

findings.title: As-of-T query construction is addable but lacks boundary and alias validation
findings.evidence: `sources.py` accepts arbitrary Europe PMC query strings, so appending `FIRST_PDATE:[1900-01-01 TO T-12-31]` is mechanically addable, and the spike reports monotonic spot checks. But `cooccur_count` builds OR groups from `program_terms`; the proposed helper contract does not specify validation of T, parenthesization, escaping, publication records with missing/changed first-publication dates, or tests that aliases and canonical disease strings are stable. `entity_maps.py` supplies one disease label per each of 12 eligible diseases and broad program synonym lists.
findings.concern_path: A syntactically accepted ceiling can still yield biased historical counts through metadata backfills, term ambiguity, or label choice; these errors affect gates, labels, and the literature baseline simultaneously and will not be exposed by monotonicity alone.
findings.suggested_action: Add golden-query tests, cutoff-boundary tests, query-receipt persistence, and predefined disease/gene alias handling before the sweep.

findings.title: Live Europe PMC cannot satisfy the promised value-identical rerun without freezing responses
findings.evidence: Build step 6 requires value-identical reruns while “Where it runs” calls Europe PMC live. `sources.py` delegates to an HTTP cache, but the plan only says caches are gitignored and does not define a frozen corpus snapshot, cache manifest, retrieval timestamp, response hashes, or archival artifact. Europe PMC can backfill records and metadata even for pre-2016 dates.
findings.concern_path: A clean rerun on another machine or after cache eviction can change `ab`, `bc`, `ac_lit_asof`, current labels, gates, rankings, and metrics. A private gitignored cache is not a reproducible research receipt.
findings.suggested_action: Freeze and hash every query/result with retrieval date and publish a non-sensitive count manifest sufficient to rerun metrics without live API drift.

findings.title: Time-invariant referee is not circular, but “prediction” needs stricter temporal wording
findings.evidence: `referee_triple.py` reads fixed local referee tables and exact-C disease enrichment at FDR<0.05; it does not call Europe PMC or read the future-literature label. Thus direct label leakage is not shown. However, the substrate is a 2025 experiment used to rank literature changes beginning after 2016, so this is not a model or evidence source that existed at T.
findings.concern_path: Calling the result prospective prediction can imply the 2025 data forecast events from 2016 onward, even though much of the outcome literature may predate the experiment and may have influenced the experimental study’s design or disease gene-set annotations. The comparison can still test association with later literature, but not historical deployability.
findings.suggested_action: State the estimand as retrospective literature time-slicing with a contemporary fixed substrate and audit whether the disease-enrichment tables incorporate literature-derived annotations overlapping the outcome.

findings.title: Referee-supported-first may dominate through disease-hop construction rather than the claimed three-hop combination
findings.evidence: The stage-1 funnel shows the exact-C referee collapses 22,039 eligible pairs to only 43 supported variants, overwhelmingly through 21,995 `refuted_for_c` classifications. In `referee_triple.py`, exact-C support is determined by membership in a disease-enriched cluster gene set at FDR<0.05. The proposed effect-only and enrichment-only baselines do not clearly reproduce the binary disease-hop gate used by Wayfinder.
findings.concern_path: If future gene-disease co-mention is strongly associated with membership in disease-annotated gene sets, supported-first can win almost entirely from the exact-C disease hop. That is not circular with Europe PMC, but it does not demonstrate that the full referee or gene→program→disease combination adds value beyond its disease-hop component.
findings.suggested_action: Define an exact disease-hop-only baseline using the same support statistic and compare incremental value of QC/effect/program signals without changing the core design.

findings.title: Adaptive fallback permits outcome-informed redesign
findings.evidence: Build step 2 says that if positives fall below 100, “widen T or relax the novelty threshold and re-size; record the decision.” Those choices alter the target population after labels have been computed. The same artifact already includes multiple T values, establishment thresholds, and novelty definitions.
findings.concern_path: A weak primary setup can be replaced after observing label prevalence—and potentially performance—creating researcher degrees of freedom that undermine the claimed honest-null commitment.
findings.suggested_action: Predefine a blinded feasibility rule based only on label counts, a fixed ordered fallback sequence, and prohibit inspecting method performance until the final frame is locked.

findings.title: “Established-now ≥5 co-mentions” is an unvalidated and popularity-confounded outcome
findings.evidence: The spike calls ≥5 co-mentions “defensibly written down,” but offers only examples and no manual validation sample. Europe PMC queries use quoted free-text gene and disease terms; disease prevalence, gene name ambiguity, publication volume, and broad program vocabulary can drive counts. The plan itself concedes this is not biological truth.
findings.concern_path: Wayfinder’s effect and enrichment signals may correlate with well-studied genes or diseases, so success could measure subsequent publication popularity rather than mechanistic link establishment. A literature-rarity baseline alone does not control disease and gene popularity.
findings.suggested_action: Add blinded manual validation of a stratified label sample and popularity-matched baselines or stratified metrics by gene and disease publication volume.

convergence_status: round 1 — no prior position to converge with
recommended_next_question: Claude should first resolve F-001 and F-002 with an exact, executable definition of the common novel-at-T ranking frame and Wayfinder’s total ordering over every candidate, then justify the paired inference and frozen primary estimand.
